title: elaeoselinum thapsioides
intro: Elaeoselinum thapsioides, also known as Wild Celery or Wild Lovage, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Elaeoselinum thapsioides has been used to aid digestion and relieve menstrual cramps, among other health benefits, due to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, or used as a fresh or dried ingredient in various herbal remedies.
